name: Nilearn (Machine Learning for Neuroimaging)

auth.notes: Local library; no auth required; fetches datasets from public repositories

performance.notes: CPU-bound; GLM and connectivity analyses can be slow for large datasets

agent_readiness.notes: Machine learning and statistics for fMRI/sMRI neuroimaging. from nilearn import image, plotting, maskers, datasets. img = image.load_img('fmri.nii.gz'). fmri_img = image.smooth_img(img, fwhm=6). masker = maskers.NiftiMasker(mask_img=mask). X = masker.fit_transform(fmri_img). plotting.plot_stat_map(stat_img). plotting.view_img(img) for interactive. GLM: first_level_model.fit(fmri_img, events). Connectivity: ConnectivityMeasure. Datasets: datasets.fetch_atlas_harvard_oxford(). Decoders: SVM/Ridge on brain images.

gotchas: Steep learning curve for neuroscience concepts (GLM, HRF, connectivity)
Large datasets (full brain 4D fMRI) require careful memory management
best_when: fMRI GLM analysis and connectivity analysis
Brain decoding (predicting cognitive states from fMRI)
Brain atlas manipulation and ROI extraction
avoid_when: Non-fMRI imaging (use nibabel/ITK for structural MRI)
Deep learning on brain data (use TorchIO/MONAI)
summary: Nilearn provides ML and statistics for fMRI neuroimaging. NiftiMasker().fit_transform(fmri_img) extracts time series. GLM: FirstLevelModel for task fMRI analysis. Connectivity: ConnectivityMeasure for resting-state. Brain decoding: SVM/Ridge on fMRI data. Brain atlas fetching. plotting.view_img() for interactive. BSD-3 by INRIA. The standard Python library for fMRI machine learning and connectivity analysis.
